rationale: This page provides comprehensive details on the Lokpal institution, including disqualification criteria for members, the composition of its selection committee, the functional structure of its two wings (Inquiry and Prosecution), and its legal powers. This is high-value content for polity and governance exams.
cleanedText:
Disqualifications for Chairperson or Member
* MP or Legislature Member: Cannot be a Member of Parliament or Legislature of any State or Union territory.
* Convicted of Moral Turpitude.
* Age Requirement: Cannot be a person of less than 45 years of age on the date of assuming office.
* Panchayat or Municipality Member.
* Removed or Dismissed from Service.
* Office of Trust or Profit.
Composition of Selection Committee of Lokpal
* Prime Minister (Chairperson)
* Speaker of House of the People (Member)
* Leader of Opposition in House of the People (Member)
* Chief Justice of India or a Judge of Supreme Court nominated by him (Member)
* One eminent jurist, to be nominated by President (Member)
Two wings of Lokpal
* Inquiry Wing:
* Conducts preliminary inquiry into offences by public servants under PoCA, 1988.
* Submits inquiry report to Lokpal within 60 days.
* Headed by Director of Inquiry under Lokpal Chairperson.
* Assisted by 3 SPs (General, Economic & Banking, Cyber) and inquiry officers.
* Vested with civil court powers under CPC, 1908.
* Prosecution Wing:
* Headed by Director of Prosecution.
* Files cases before Special Courts based on investigation reports.
* Prosecutes public servants for offences punishable under PCA, 1988.
Powers of Lokpal:
* Superintendence over Investigations: Exercises supervision and direction over central investigation agencies, including the CBI, for cases referred by Lokpal.
* Document Protection: Can issue directions to public servants for preservation and protection of records/documents.
* Civil Court Powers: Possesses powers under the Code of Civil Procedure to conduct preliminary inquiry and investigation into corruption cases.
* Appellate Authority: Acts as the final appellate authority in public grievance cases involving findings of corruption under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.
